description: Hard constraints enforced at generation time. The AI must not violate any of these rules. These are derived from sports science principles documented in the knowledge base.

traditional.format: Sets x Reps with rest period
traditional.use_case: Peak intensity weeks, maximal loads, psychological readiness variation

emom.description: Every Minute/2 Minutes/3 Minutes On the Minute
emom.rule: Mix EMOM and traditional formats. Use EMOM for 30-50% of strength sessions.
emom.rest_adequacy: Interval must allow phosphocreatine recovery. Heavy loads (>80% 1RM) require minimum E2MOM. Maximal loads (>90% 1RM) require E3MOM.

progression_schemes.description: Intra-session loading progression patterns. Must be justified in loading_why rationale.

straight_sets.description: Same weight and reps across all sets
straight_sets.best_for: Hypertrophy, baseline strength, simple movements, novice athletes
straight_sets.when_to_use: Week 1-2 baseline phases, moderate intensity work
straight_sets.justification_required: Explain why constant load is appropriate for this session's goal

ramp_buildup.description: Progressively increase load across sets while decreasing reps
ramp_buildup.best_for: Neural preparation, technique development, complex movements
ramp_buildup.when_to_use: Week 1 of programs, Olympic lifting sessions, athletes returning from layoff
ramp_buildup.justification_required: Explain why progressive neural preparation is needed (e.g., movement complexity, Week 1 technique focus)
ramp_buildup.reference: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training

wave_loading.description: Oscillate between heavier and lighter loads
wave_loading.best_for: Strength emphasis, PAP exploitation, experienced athletes
wave_loading.when_to_use: Week 2-3 of programs, heavy compound lifts
wave_loading.justification_required: Explain why PAP exploitation is appropriate (e.g., Week 2-3 intensity phase, neural adaptation emphasis)

top_set_backoff.description: One heavy top set followed by lighter volume sets
top_set_backoff.best_for: Time efficiency, peak intensity + volume, concurrent training
top_set_backoff.when_to_use: Week 3 peak intensity, intermediate athletes, limited time sessions
top_set_backoff.justification_required: Explain why combining peak intensity and volume work is appropriate (e.g., Week 3 intensity peak, concurrent training efficiency)

rules.variety_required: Do not use the same progression scheme for all strength sessions in a program
rules.match_to_context: Scheme must match training week, movement complexity, and athlete level
rules.justification_mandatory: Every strength block must explain in loading_why rationale WHY this specific progression scheme was chosen

week_guidelines.week_1: Prefer straight sets or ramp/buildup for baseline establishment
week_guidelines.week_2: Mix schemes - introduce wave loading for strength emphasis sessions
week_guidelines.week_3: Use top set + backoff for peak intensity sessions, wave loading for compounds
week_guidelines.week_4_deload: Straight sets only at reduced intensity

sequencing_forbidden: heavy posterior chain after heavy squat
olympic lifting after heavy lower body
two For Time metcons on consecutive days
two high-CNS sessions back to back

allowed_sources: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training
Schoenfeld - Science and Development of Muscle Hypertrophy
Wilson et al 2012 - Concurrent Training Meta-Analysis
Behm et al 2016 - Acute Effects of Muscle Stretching
Leetun et al 2004 - Core Stability Measures as Risk Factors for Lower Extremity Injury in Athletes
Silva et al 2017 - High-Intensity Interval Training and Pacing Strategies

safety.ghd_situp_note: Introduce GHD sit-ups gradually — causes severe DOMS in untrained athletes. Cap at 10 reps in first exposure.
safety.olympic_technique_note: Olympic lifting prescriptions must include a technique note. Load must not exceed what allows technically sound execution.
safety.kipping_prerequisite: Do not prescribe kipping pull-ups without noting strict pull-up prerequisite (3+ strict reps minimum).

equipment_restrictions.note: PVC pipes are not commonly available. Use 'empty barbell' or 'broomstick' for mobility work instead.

movement_descriptions.rule: Every movement must have clear, concise notes explaining what it is if the name is not self-evident. Use the 'notes' field to add brief descriptions (e.g., 'Hip opener stretch', 'Shoulder mobility drill').
